channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Após saída de Ratinho Júnior, ala do PSD de Kassab faz ‘jogo de cena’ nos bastidores
item.description: The PSD, led by Gilberto Kassab, maintains its own presidential pre-candidacy even after Ratinho Júnior withdrew from the race, a move seen internally as a 'staged performance' to boost Kassab's bargaining power in state-level politics. Although Kassab has publicly reaffirmed the intention to launch a competitive candidate — such as Ronaldo Caiado or Eduardo Leite — there is skepticism about the candidacy’s viability, with the possibility of abandoning the race and freeing party members to support other candidates, like Lula or a right-wing figure, if the effort fails to gain traction.

item.title: Leite diz que candidatura à presidência é única opção e defende que PSD seja 'centro que está faltando no Brasil'
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite stated that his presidential candidacy is his only political option and that, if not running, he will remain in office until the end of his term. He argues the PSD should be the 'center that is missing in Brazil' and met today in São Paulo with party president Gilberto Kassab to solidify support. These talks follow the withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior from the PSD's internal process to define its presidential candidate.

item.title: Comissão do Senado aprova novo Plano Nacional de Educação e texto segue para o plenário
item.description: The Senate Education Committee approved the new National Education Plan (PNE) without substantive changes, only with wording adjustments. The text now proceeds to a vote in the plenary and, if approved, will be sent to President Lula for sanction. The PNE sets goals for literacy, full-time schooling, connectivity, and school infrastructure over the next decade.

item.title: Plenário aprova criação de novos cargos na Justiça Eleitoral
item.description: The Senate Plenary approved PL 4/2024, which creates 232 judicial analyst positions, 242 judicial technician roles, 75 commissioned positions, and 245 commissioned functions in the TSE and TREs, including 117 vacancies for the DF TRE and 85 for the TSE. The bill, already passed by the Chamber of Deputies, now awaits presidential sanction and was justified as essential to meet growing voter numbers and rising candidacies, strengthening the institutional capacity of Electoral Justice. The vote was symbolic, with opposing votes from Eduardo Girão, Hamilton Mourão, and Damares Alves.

item.title: Bolsonaro pede autorização para entrada de 12 funcionários em casa durante prisão
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro requested authorization from Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to allow eight lawyers and 12 staff members to enter his residence during his humanitarian house arrest. The list includes Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, his son and a pre-candidate, among other legal advisors. The request aims to secure legal and logistical support during the restriction of liberty period.

item.title: Congresso aprova aumento de vagas na Justiça Eleitoral; impacto previsto é de R$ 109 milhões
item.description: Congress approved a bill to create 794 new positions in the Electoral Justice system, with an estimated annual impact of R$ 109.3 million, as calculated by the TSE. The positions include judicial analysts, technicians, commissioned roles, and trust positions, aimed at addressing voter growth, increased candidacies, and new challenges like disinformation and electoral security. Funding will come from existing budget allocations, subject to approval in the LDO and annual budget law.

item.title: Quase 82 milhões de brasileiros estão inadimplentes
item.description: President Lula linked high household indebtedness to declining government popularity, despite low unemployment and rising income. Serasa Experian data shows 81.7 million Brazilians are delinquent — near a historical record — with a 38.1% increase over ten years and disproportionate impact on women and the elderly. Demand for debt swapping is growing, but experts warn that without financial education, the debt cycle persists, merely replacing high-cost debt with longer-term obligations.

item.title: PGR se opõe a Fux e defende regras para eleição indireta no Rio
item.description: The Attorney General's Office (PGR) opposed a decision by Supreme Court Justice Luiz Fux that suspended parts of a rule for indirect elections in Rio de Janeiro, established by state deputies. The PGR argues the state law should be upheld, claiming judicial intervention undermines federal autonomy. The dispute arises ahead of upcoming indirect elections in the state.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro supera Lula em possível 2º turno, diz pesquisa
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) surpassed President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) in a potential runoff scenario, according to an AtlasIntel-Bloomberg poll released on March 25, 2025, with 47.6% to 46.6%, within the margin of error. Since December, Bolsonaro gained 10.8 percentage points while Lula lost 2 points. In first-round simulations, Lula leads with 46% across all scenarios, while Flávio ranges between 36% and 42%. The poll has a margin of error of 1 percentage point.

item.title: Após inelegibilidade, Ciro Nogueira convida Cláudio Castro para se filiar ao PP
item.description: Ciro Nogueira, president of the Progressistas party, publicly invited former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro to join the party following his ineligibility ruling by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power. Castro, who had Senate ambitions, is barred from running in elections until 2030 due to his conviction over irregular hiring schemes involving R$ 248 million. Nogueira expressed confidence that justice would prevail and restore Castro’s political future.

item.title: Os riscos em mudar o trabalho nos aplicativos neste momento ‘pauta populista’
item.description: Economist Alex Agostini warns that hasty decisions on app-based work regulation and reducing the workweek (such as the 6×1 schedule) could backfire if they ignore Brazil’s reality, where these platforms serve as a vital income source for millions in a labor market with excess supply. He advocates for balance between worker protections and economic viability, cautioning that unilateral, populist-driven rules — especially in an election year — may raise consumer costs or reduce worker opportunities. Fiscal transparency could boost government revenue, but risks passing costs to drivers or users.

item.title: STF continua julgamento sobre privatização da Sabesp após destaque indevido
item.description: The STF resumed the trial of actions challenging Sabesp's privatization after correcting an improper spotlight request by Minister Luiz Fux. So far, two votes support maintaining privatization: those of rapporteur Cristiano Zanin and Minister Gilmar Mendes. The virtual session ends on March 27.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula visita centro de manutenção da Latam em São Carlos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva visited the Latam maintenance center in São Carlos (SP), the largest in South America, which is undergoing expansion focused on research, development, and innovation, including aircraft modernization and digital maintenance solutions. The visit also included the inauguration of new areas at the UFSCar University Hospital, highlighting the administration's commitment to regional health and development.

item.title: TSE determina cassação imediata do deputado estadual Rodrigo Bacellar
item.description: The TSE ordered the immediate revocation of state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar’s (União) mandate, president of Alerj, for abuse of political power in the 2022 elections. In addition to losing his office, he was declared ineligible by a 5-2 vote, following the same ruling applied to former governor Cláudio Castro. Alerj must hold a new election for its presidency within six sessions.

item.title: Imagem de Flávio Bolsonaro abraçado a Vorcaro é falsa e foi criada por IA
item.description: The image showing Flávio Bolsonaro embracing Daniel Vorcaro is false and was generated by artificial intelligence, as verified by UOL Confere using tools like SynthID and Hive Moderation, which detected Google Gemini watermarks. Justice had previously ordered the removal of similar content in March, with fines set for reposting.

item.title: PGR se posiciona contra Fux e defende regras da Alerj para eleição indireta ao Governo do RJ
item.description: The PGR opposed the rules set by a preliminary injunction from Supreme Court Minister Luiz Fux regarding indirect elections for the governor of Rio de Janeiro, advocating instead for the rules established by the Alerj as more appropriate. The position was taken on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, aiming to ensure the legality of the indirect electoral process in the state.

item.title: Desistência de Ratinho Jr. tem pouco efeito sobre chances de Flávio
item.description: Ratinho Jr.'s withdrawal had limited impact on Flávio Bolsonaro's chances, as his likely replacement, Ronaldo Caiado, is equally competitive and may even draw anti-Lula voters away from Bolsonaro. Despite Flávio's strong early campaign performance and regional influence, the substitution of opponents does not significantly alter the electoral landscape. The potential nomination of a centrist candidate like Eduardo Leite could revive the argument that Bolsonaro's radicalism would be highlighted, but this remains conditional.

item.title: Lula critica ausência de Tarcísio em evento de fábrica de trens em Araraquara
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the absence of São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas at the inauguration ceremony of the Chinese CRRC train factory in Araraquara. Lula emphasized the importance of state-level participation in infrastructure projects that drive job creation and regional development. Tarcísio’s absence was interpreted as a sign of political distancing between the federal and São Paulo state governments.

item.title: A duração do processo eleitoral e seus impactos institucionais que comprometem a democracia
item.description: The prolonged duration of electoral judicial processes in Brazil undermines the effectiveness of the Electoral Justice and public trust in democratic institutions. The case of Roraima Governor Antonio Denarium, under trial since August 2024 for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, illustrates how judicial delays can render decisions practically ineffective, despite favorable votes for annulment. Although two votes for cassation were issued in 2025, the lack of a final ruling creates institutional strain by conflicting judicial timing with the electoral mandate, threatening electoral fairness and the legitimacy of outcomes.

item.title: Lula lamenta ausência de Tarcísio em evento e critica governador por Casa Paulista
item.description: President Lula expressed regret over Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' absence at the CRRC train factory visit in Araraquara, emphasizing the nearly R$ 7 billion federal investment in the project. He criticized the Casa Paulista program for concealing federal involvement and accused Tarcísio of erasing the name of Minha Casa, Minha Vida. Lula stated that in the 2026 elections, São Paulo voters will learn which governor truly implements social policies.

item.title: Eleição indireta no Rio: o entendimento da PGR que favorece grupo de Castro
item.description: The Attorney General's Office (PGR) deemed constitutional the bill approved by the Rio de Janeiro Legislative Assembly (Alerj) regulating the indirect election, supporting open voting and no six-month resignation requirement. Minister Luiz Fux of the STF had previously struck down these provisions, mandating secret ballot and a six-month grace period. As a result, candidates such as Douglas Ruas, Nicola Miccione, and André Ceciliano may be disqualified.

item.title: MDB diz que manterá candidatura de vice-governador do RS mesmo se Leite permanecer no cargo
item.description: The MDB stated it will maintain the candidacy of Gabriel Souza, current vice-governor of Rio Grande do Sul, for the state government in the 2026 elections, even if Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) remains in office until the end of his term. The decision suggests a potential internal rupture within the coalition or a strategy to prepare for succession, regardless of Leite’s continuity.

item.title: Lula: Brasil pode chegar a ser a sexta ou quinta economia do mundo
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that Brazil could become the fifth or sixth largest economy in the world, emphasizing its natural resources, territory, and potential for a qualitative leap in development. He argued that Brazil already possesses the necessary conditions to transition from an emerging to a developed nation, citing his visit to the CRRC train factory in Araraquara and the signing of BNDES financing contracts worth R$ 5.6 billion.

item.title: Kataguiri pede quebra de sigilo bancário de Moraes e Viviane
item.description: Federal deputy Kim Kataguiri filed two requests with the INSS CPI seeking to lift banking and fiscal secrecy for Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes and his wife, Viviane Barci de Moraes, to investigate R$80.2 million she received from Banco Master, linked to Daniel Vorcaro, for services rendered over 22 months. The requests aim to verify possible patrimonial communion between the couple and whether funds were used to acquire assets in the minister’s name, based on contract details circulating since December 2025. The move includes a request to Coaf for Financial Intelligence Reports (RIFs) and unfolds as the STF reviews the continuation of the INSS CPI, whose final report was initially scheduled for March 28, 2026.

item.title: A resposta do STF à CPMI do INSS após cobrança sobre telefone ligado a mensagens de Vorcaro
item.description: The STF asked the INSS CPI to clarify the purpose of its request regarding a functional phone allegedly linked to messages from Daniel Vorcaro, former CEO of Banco Master. The STF's general directorate said that, depending on the motivation, the analysis could be handled by President Edson Fachin. Senator Carlos Viana claimed that Vorcaro's messages in November 2025 were sent to a STF number, according to the Telephone and Telematic Records Investigation System.

item.title: Gilvan da Federal vira réu no STF por ataques ao comandante do Exército
item.description: Federal Deputy Gilvan da Federal (PL-ES) has been made a defendant by the Supreme Federal Court (STF) for slander, defamation, and libel against Army Commander General Tomás Paiva, following remarks made in plenary session and a video in November 2025. The First Turma of the STF rejected the parliamentary immunity claim, stating that insults are not constitutionally protected. The case will now proceed as a criminal action, potentially leading to conviction or acquittal.

item.title: Flávio pula na frente de Lula
item.description: The pre-campaign of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) is gaining momentum, according to an AtlasIntel poll released on Wednesday, the 25th, showing him in a competitive position against President Lula. Meanwhile, Lula’s allies accuse GloboNews of bias after the dismissal of an editor allegedly due to government pressure, fueling narratives of victimization among supporters. The article contrasts the perceived weakness of the government with the rising traction of Flávio Bolsonaro’s candidacy.

item.title: “Bandidos não vão mais decidir eleições”, diz Van Hatten após sanção do PL Antifacção
item.description: Federal Deputy Marcel van Hatten (Novo-RS) celebrated the sanction of the Anti-Faction Bill, emphasizing its role in combating organized crime and highlighting an amendment that restricts voting rights for incarcerated individuals. He stated the measure reflects his party's efforts to transform Brazil, declaring that 'criminals will no longer decide elections'.

item.title: Lula detona ausência de Tarcísio de Freitas em lançamento de fábrica de trens em SP
item.description: President Lula criticized the absence of São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas at the inauguration of a Chinese train factory in Araraquara, emphasizing the 7 billion reais investment and its potential to generate jobs and modernity. He also stated that the 2026 elections will be a contest between 'truth' and 'lie'.

item.title: MDB orienta Lula a formalizar indicação de Messias e presidente deve ligar para Alcolumbre
item.description: Lula was advised by MDB members to formally nominate Jorge Messias to the STF and committed to calling Senate President Davi Alcolumbre by the 26th to resolve the impasse. The lack of communication between Alcolumbre and government leader Jaques Wagner hinders the nomination process, although MDB senators assess the current scenario as more favorable than last year.

item.title: Boulos diz que há omissão de governadores no ICMS dos combustíveis
item.description: Guilherme Boulos, Secretary-General of the Presidency, accused governors of failing to reduce ICMS on fuels, particularly diesel, and blamed recent price hikes on the greed of distributors and gas stations, despite federal tax exemptions. He made these remarks after a meeting with truckers at the Planalto Palace, linking the situation to the trade war initiated by Donald Trump against Iran. The federal government announced it will expand oversight by ANP, PF, Senacon, and Procons to combat abusive practices in the fuel supply chain.

item.title: Lula diz lamentar ausência de Tarcísio em evento em Araraquara e diz que eleição de 2026 será disputa entre 'verdade e mentira'
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva regretted the absence of São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas at an event for the installation of a Chinese train factory in Araraquara, SP, noting his absence was felt during the ceremony. Lula stated that the 2026 election will be a contest between 'truth and lies,' underscoring the deepening political polarization in Brazil.

item.title: Advogado de Bolsonaro assume defesa do cunhado de Vorcaro
item.description: Fabiano Zettel, brother-in-law of Daniel Vorcaro and detained in Operation Compliance Zero, replaced his defense lawyers with Celso Vilardi, who also defends Jair Bolsonaro. The change suggests Zettel may accept a plea deal, similar to Vorcaro, whose potential agreement is highly anticipated in Brasília given his extensive ties across the three branches of government.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula inaugura novas áreas de hospital em São Carlos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva inaugurates on Wednesday (March 25, 2026) the expansion of the Federal University of São Carlos University Hospital, adding new medical and surgical beds, operating rooms, and a Nephrology Unit, funded by the Novo PAC. The expansion aims to increase SUS healthcare capacity and strengthen health professional training.

item.title: Primeiro caça Gripen fabricado no Brasil é apresentado e país ingressa em seleto clube
item.description: Brazil unveiled its first domestically manufactured F-39E Gripen fighter jet, joining the select group of nations capable of producing supersonic combat aircraft. President Lula attended the ceremony without speaking, merely christening the aircraft, while the Defense Minister emphasized the contract for 36 jets, technology transfer, and maintenance autonomy. Launched in 2014, the program has so far spent R$ 16.75 billion and is now scheduled for completion by 2032, with 15 aircraft to be built at Embraer’s facility in Gavião Peixoto.

item.title: Boulos: caminhoneiros não podem pagar o preço de governadores não mexerem no ICMS
item.description: Minister of the General Secretariat of the Presidency, Guilherme Boulos, criticized governors for not reducing the ICMS on fuels, accusing them of omission amid rising diesel prices driven by artificial increases by distributors and the U.S.-Iran conflict. He said the government will maintain a permanent dialogue with truckers while the provisional measure on minimum freight rates is debated in Congress, strengthening fuel station inspections and ruling out strikes, as prior demands have already been met.

item.title: Eduardo Leite rejeita papel de vice em candidatura ao Planalto
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul and member of the PSD, rejected the possibility of running as vice president on a presidential ticket and stated he is working to make his own candidacy for the Planalto Palace viable in the 2026 elections. The comment was made on Wednesday, the 25th, during internal PSD discussions, emphasizing his intent to lead a majoritarian ticket.

item.title: Cortejado pelo PSB, Pacheco janta com João Campos nesta 4ª feira
item.description: Recife mayor and national PSB president João Campos will dine with Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) in Brasília on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, to persuade Pacheco to join the PSB and run for governor of Minas Gerais, thereby securing a political base for President Lula in the state. Pacheco cannot remain in the PSD due to the allegiance of Vice Governor Mateus Simões (also PSD) to Governor Romeu Zema (Novo). The party-switching window without risk of mandate loss extends until April 3, 2026.

item.title: Flávio, Carlos e Michelle recalculam rota após decisão sobre domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Allies of Jair Bolsonaro expect the house arrest granted by the STF to be extended throughout the electoral period to prevent his return to prison from being exploited as a political weapon by the opposition. The strategy aims to allow Michelle Bolsonaro to resume her nationwide travels as head of PL Women, strengthening the party's female base, while Carlos Bolsonaro acts as a liaison between the former president and the party leadership.

item.title: TSE determina cassação imediata de mandato de Rodrigo Bacellar, presidente da Alerj
item.description: The TSE ordered the immediate removal of Rodrigo Bacellar's mandate, suspended president of the Alerj, in a decision issued on Wednesday (25). The ruling impacts the leadership of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislative Assembly and underscores efforts to combat administrative misconduct. The case is linked to investigations into conduct incompatible with the exercise of parliamentary office.

item.title: A trajetória do último dos gigantes pela luta dos direitos civis nos EUA
item.description: The burial of Reverend Jesse Jackson marks the end of an era in the U.S. civil rights movement, recognized as the last of the giants who transformed Black political inclusion in America. His leadership inspired figures like Barack Obama and Kamala Harris, solidified diversity in political discourse, and encouraged civic engagement through voting and boycotts of discriminatory practices. In Brazil, he left a legacy by supporting the creation of Zumbi dos Palmares University and the Corporate Initiative for Racial Equality.

item.title: Oposição teme que Bolsonaro em casa interfira mais em campanha de Flávio e dificulte acordos
item.description: Opposition figures welcomed the decision by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to place former President Jair Bolsonaro under house arrest, but fear his continued presence at home could interfere more directly in the presidential campaign of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, complicating political alliances. While seen as a blow to bolsonarism, the move raises concerns about increased behind-the-scenes influence from the former president in the 2026 election.

item.title: Leite: PSD terá de decidir se é partido que defende a anistia ou fala de um Brasil diferente
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite states that the choice of the PSD's presidential pre-candidate in 2026 will be decisive in determining whether the party defends amnesty or pursues a vision of a different Brazil. He emphasizes that this decision goes beyond a name and reflects the ideological identity the PSD will assume before the country. The statement comes amid internal debates over the party's direction in the face of national polarization.

item.title: ‘Burocrático, chato e enfadonho’: Enquanto direita avança, juventude francesa reduz participação nas eleições
item.description: French youth is increasingly disengaged from politics, perceiving it as bureaucratic and ineffective, contributing to a turnout of around 57% in the 2026 municipal elections — the second-highest abstention since 1958. While cities like Lyon, Paris, and Marseille re-elected left-wing mayors by narrow margins, the right advanced nationally, winning in Toulouse and securing 93 municipalities, signaling a conservative surge that contrasts with the political engagement seen in countries like Brazil.

item.title: CEO da Saab vê Brasil como polo regional de caças supersônicos
item.description: Saab CEO Micael Johansson stated that Brazil has the potential to become a regional hub for supersonic fighters, emphasizing the production of the F-39E Gripen by Embraer in Gavião Peixoto. He noted that this delivery is just the beginning, with local capabilities ensuring continuous evolution and operational excellence for decades. The event featured federal and state authorities along with Swedish representatives, marking a milestone for Latin America's defense industry.

item.title: Bolsonaro tem reclamado de dor no ombro direito e médicos indicam cirurgia
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro is hospitalized at DF Star Hospital in Brasília for bilateral pneumonia and has reported right shoulder pain, with medical specialists indicating a need for surgical intervention. However, the surgery is being postponed due to his ongoing recovery from pneumonia, and he is expected to be discharged this Friday (27) after completing his antibiotic course. Bolsonaro, sentenced to 27 years in prison for attempting a coup, is currently under house arrest granted by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, under strict conditions including an electronic ankle monitor and bans on cell phones and social media.

item.title: PT vota a favor de empréstimo pedido por Tarcísio, e bolsonarista critica
item.description: The São Paulo Legislative Assembly (Alesp) approved a bill proposed by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas authorizing a R$15 billion loan, with support from PT deputies. Bolsonarist state deputy Gil Diniz (PL) criticized the vote, accusing PT members of acting as part of the governor’s governing base.

item.title: Em Ribeirão Preto, Zema descarta ser vice e mira segundo turno com campanha anti-PT
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo), pre-candidate for the presidency in 2026, ruled out being a running mate and said he will maintain his pre-candidacy until the end, emphasizing his distinct proposals. He pledged active support for any right-wing candidate if he doesn’t reach the runoff and reiterated his anti-PT campaign, accusing the party of hindering Brazil’s development with outdated ideas. Zema noted he currently has 4% to 5% in voting intentions and is confident of growth as he travels across the country.

item.title: Lula x Flávio: o resultado de ‘forte peso simbólico’ na nova pesquisa AtlasIntel
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro leading Lula in a hypothetical runoff with 47.6% to 46.6%, within the margin of error, indicating a technical tie. Flávio’s gain is attributed to a strong transfer of votes from Jair Bolsonaro, while Lula has lost about 6.4 points since December due to unfavorable national and international contexts. Despite the symbolic lead, analysts stress the race remains tight and undecided, with the politically disengaged middle electorate likely to determine the outcome.

item.title: [Coluna] Fazendo retrospectiva de um país complicado
item.description: A German columnist for DW Brasil ends her monthly column after eight years due to German government budget cuts. She reflects on her time in Brazil, noting the polarized reader engagement — especially around Bolsonaro — and observes that despite widespread consensus on parliamentary corruption, no meaningful change occurs.
